page.content: The first table presents data on pre-transplant CMV serology with a total of 208 patients analyzed. The D−/R− group consists of 58 patients (28%), with 55 (26.4%) experiencing CMV episodes, including 1 (0.5%) with one episode and 2 (1.0%) with two or more episodes. The D−/R+ group includes 59 patients (28.5%), with 28 (13.5%) developing CMV episodes; 19 (9.1%) had one episode and 12 (5.8%) had two or more episodes.

cells.cell_text: Age of semen collection entry, d

page.content: In the D+/R− cohort of 20 patients (9.5%), 15 (7.2%) faced CMV episodes, comprising 4 (1.9%) with one and 1 (0.5%) with two or more episodes. The D+/R+ group of 71 patients (34%) recorded 40 (19.2%) with CMV episodes, including 20 (9.6%) having one episode and 11 (5.3%) having two or more episodes. The overall totals indicate 138 patients (66.4%) had CMV episodes, with 44 (21.2%) having one episode and 26 (12.5%) having two or more episodes. The second table includes traits such as the age of semen collection entry with a mean of 259.74 days and standard deviation of 33.51, ranging from 201 to 458 days; boar herd life averaging 835.57 days (SD 503.5, Min 21, Max 2350); daily gain at 775.81 g (SD 94.32, Min 554, Max 1170); lean meat content at 61.34% (SD 1.94, Min 55, Max 67); and ejaculates incompatible with requirements averaging 3.36 (SD 5.49, Min 0, Max 37).
